top_signals.title: Oil Swings as US, Iran Continue Talks After Renewed Fighting
top_signals.summary: Oil fluctuated between gains and losses as the US and Iran continued talks following a flare-up in attacks that strained their ceasefire and deterred tanker traffic through the Strait of Hormuz.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Stocks Mixed Before SK Hynix’s Debut as Oil Swings: Markets Wrap
top_signals.summary: A rally that drove stocks to a striking distance of their record highs wavered ahead of the earnings season, with traders also parsing geopolitical developments as they get ready for SK Hynix Inc.’s debut.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Inside NATO's extraordinary 48 hours that revealed Trump's grip on global diplomacy
top_signals.summary: Steve Sedgwick goes behind the scenes of NATO's dramatic 48 hours, where Trump's changing tone reshaped diplomacy and left allies guessing.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Murder inquiry launched into death of former Tory minister Ann Widdecombe
top_signals.summary: Devon and Cornwall Police say Widdecombe, 78, sustained ‘serious injuries’
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Pubs to pour five million extra pints during England v Norway World Cup clash
top_signals.summary: Pubs are set to score their biggest boost yet for the World Cup when they pour more than five million extra pints during England’s game against Norway this Saturday. As many as 5.5m more pints will be served to thirsty England fans while the Three Lions face off in the quarter final in Miami, bringing [...]

top_signals.title: Battersea Power Station misreporting claims scrutinised by accounting watchdog
top_signals.summary: The City’s accounting watchdog is scrutinising claims of financial misreporting at Battersea Power Station after the former chief executive alleged he was dismissed from his role for blowing the whistle on inflated property valuations, City AM understands. The Financial Reporting Council (FRC) is reportedly looking at claims made by the former boss of the iconic [...]

top_signals.title: Japanese scientists identify neural mechanism that may explain why we dislike people
top_signals.summary: University of Tokyo researchers said the study's findings could advance understanding of disorders in which social memory and emotional processing become distorted.
